body_txt: For added atmosphere, play this full "Psycho" Strings while reading on. A common audio cue used when you want to establish that something or someone is deeply insane, evil, or unnatural, but the Ominous Latin Chanting is off on vacation: a series of sharp, screeching notes on any string instrument. Sometimes this is paired with the Vertigo Effect . When done on strings, like in Alfred Hitchcock 's Psycho , it is frequently used by three short, fast bow strokes below the bridge — the wooden piece that holds the strings up — of a cello. note Yes, they used a Cello in Psycho . For comedic effect, most will reference a violin instead. In most horror movies, if it's not strings, it's probably a waterphone . Usually part of a "Psycho" Shower Murder Parody . See also Scare Chord and Drone of Dread for the deeper pitched cousin. Compare Orchestral Bombing and Playing the Heart Strings for string music that's dark in different ways. And yes, this is a variety of sting , or a sharp musical note played to enhance drama.
